SEARCH
Package server.subscription

Class SubscriptionRegistry

java.lang.Object
server.subscription.SubscriptionRegistry
public final class SubscriptionRegistry extends Object
Process-wide registry mapping each SubscriptionKey to the set of ClientSessions that want change events for it.

Lookups, mutations, and publishes are all thread-safe: the outer map is a ConcurrentHashMap and each per-key bucket is a CopyOnWriteArraySet, so the publish path can iterate a bucket without locking while subscribers come and go on other threads.

Publish-tolerates-broken-subscribers contract: when publish(common.dto.SubscriptionKey, common.dto.ServerEvent) fans an event out, an IOException from any one ClientSession.sendEvent(common.dto.ServerEvent) is logged and swallowed; the loop continues to the next subscriber. A single dead connection must never block notifications to the rest of the subscriber set.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static SubscriptionRegistry
    getInstance()
     
    void
    publish(common.dto.SubscriptionKey key, common.dto.ServerEvent event)
    Fans event out to every session subscribed to key.
    void
    register(ClientSession session, common.dto.SubscriptionKey key)
    Records that session wants events for key, both in the shared registry bucket and on the session itself (so disconnect cleanup can find every key the session held).
    void
    unregister(ClientSession session, common.dto.SubscriptionKey key)
    Reverses a previous register(server.net.ClientSession, common.dto.SubscriptionKey).
    void
    unregisterAll(ClientSession session)
    Detaches session from every subscription it currently holds.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static SubscriptionRegistry getInstance()
      Returns:
      the singleton registry instance

    • register

      public void register(ClientSession session, common.dto.SubscriptionKey key)
      Records that session wants events for key, both in the shared registry bucket and on the session itself (so disconnect cleanup can find every key the session held).
      Parameters:
      session - the client session that issued the SUBSCRIBE
      key - the resource being subscribed to

    • unregister

      public void unregister(ClientSession session, common.dto.SubscriptionKey key)
      Reverses a previous register(server.net.ClientSession, common.dto.SubscriptionKey). Drops the per-key bucket if it becomes empty so the map does not grow without bound under high churn.
      Parameters:
      session - the client session that issued the UNSUBSCRIBE
      key - the resource being unsubscribed from

    • unregisterAll

      public void unregisterAll(ClientSession session)
      Detaches session from every subscription it currently holds. Called from the server's connection-cleanup path when a client disconnects so the registry never tries to push to a closed socket.
      Parameters:
      session - the disconnecting client session

    • publish

      public void publish(common.dto.SubscriptionKey key, common.dto.ServerEvent event)
      Fans event out to every session subscribed to key. Per the publish-tolerates-broken-subscribers contract, an IOException from any one subscriber is logged and ignored — the loop continues to the next one so a single dead client cannot stall the channel.
      Parameters:
      key - the resource the change happened to
      event - the event to push to each subscriber
